Equal Credit Opportunity Act Notice and Fair Lending Disclosure
EQUAL CREDIT OPPORTUNITY ACT NOTICE — FAIRWAY INDEPENDENT MORTGAGE
Loan #6061178222 · Fairway Independent Mortgage · 02/11/2024
This Equal Credit Opportunity Act Notice is furnished to Catherine Nelson by Fairway Independent Mortgage in connection with loan number
6061178222 and the credit transaction concerning the property located at 5352 Ronald Burg Suite 014, Crawfordfurt, MD 43897. The federal Equal
Credit Opportunity Act and its implementing regulation, Regulation B, prohibit creditors from discriminating against credit applicants on certain prohibited
bases and confer upon you important rights with respect to the evaluation of your application, the disposition of your request for credit, and your access
to the reasons underlying any action taken. Fairway Independent Mortgage is committed to extending credit to all creditworthy applicants in a fair,
consistent, and lawful manner, and this disclosure summarizes the protections, obligations, and remedies that govern the credit transaction described
above as of February 11, 2024.
1. Prohibited Bases of Discrimination
The Equal Credit Opportunity Act and Regulation B make it unlawful for any creditor, including Fairway Independent Mortgage, to discriminate against an
applicant with respect to any aspect of a credit transaction on the basis of race, color, religion, national origin, sex, marital status, or age, provided the applicant
has the capacity to enter into a binding contract. These prohibited bases extend to every phase of the credit relationship, including the taking and evaluation of
applications, the establishment of terms and conditions, the administration of accounts, and the collection of amounts owed. Fairway Independent Mortgage
does not consider any prohibited basis as a negative factor in its assessment of the creditworthiness of Catherine Nelson.
It is further unlawful for a creditor to discriminate against an applicant because all or part of the applicant's income derives from any public assistance program,
or because the applicant has in good faith exercised any right under the Consumer Credit Protection Act. The federal agencies that enforce compliance with
this law may, in appropriate circumstances, consider a creditor's conduct toward applicants who exercise such rights. Accordingly, Fairway Independent
Mortgage evaluates income from public assistance and other lawful sources on the same terms applied to comparable income, and it does not penalize
Catherine Nelson for asserting any statutory right.
These protections apply uniformly regardless of the type, size, or purpose of the credit requested, whether the extension of credit is secured or unsecured, and
whether the transaction is consummated, withdrawn, or denied. Fairway Independent Mortgage maintains internal policies, training, and monitoring designed to
ensure that personnel involved in the origination, underwriting, and servicing of credit adhere to these requirements at every stage of the transaction
associated with loan number 6061178222.
2. Treatment of Marital Status
Consistent with Regulation B, Fairway Independent Mortgage will not refuse to extend credit, nor will it terminate or unfavorably alter the terms of an existing
extension of credit, solely on the basis of the marital status of Catherine Nelson, except where such consideration is expressly permitted or required by
applicable law. You may apply for credit in your own name, and a creditor may not require the signature of a spouse or other person, other than a joint
applicant, on any credit instrument if you qualify under the creditor's standards of creditworthiness for the amount and terms of the credit requested.
Where the value of jointly owned property, or property located in a community property jurisdiction, is relied upon to satisfy the creditor's standards, additional
signatures may be required to the extent necessary to make the collateral available to satisfy the debt in the event of default, consistent with the law of the
applicable MD jurisdiction. Any such requirement will be applied without regard to sex or marital status and only to the extent reasonably necessary to protect
the creditor's legitimate interest in the collateral securing loan number 6061178222.
If you are married, you have the right to have credit history that reflects accounts you and your spouse use or for which you are both liable reported in the
name of each spouse. Upon request, Fairway Independent Mortgage will furnish information to consumer reporting agencies in a manner that enables
Catherine Nelson to establish an independent credit history, subject to the accuracy of the underlying account records and the requirements of applicable law.
3. Notification of Action Taken
Regulation B requires Fairway Independent Mortgage to notify Catherine Nelson of the action taken on an application for credit within the time periods
prescribed by law, generally within thirty (30) days after receiving a completed application concerning the credit requested. The action taken may consist of an
approval, a counteroffer, an approval subject to conditions, an incomplete-application notice, or a denial. Where a counteroffer is extended and you do not
expressly accept or use the credit offered, Fairway Independent Mortgage may treat the application in accordance with the timing and notice rules established
under Regulation B.
If an application is incomplete regarding matters that Catherine Nelson can complete, Fairway Independent Mortgage may, in lieu of providing a notice of action
taken, send a written notice specifying the information needed, designating a reasonable period of time within which to furnish it, and informing you that failure
to provide the requested information will result in no further consideration of the application. The period afforded for completion will be reasonable under the
circumstances and will be communicated in the notice issued in connection with loan number 6061178222.
A notice of action taken will be provided in a form that you may retain, and it will identify Fairway Independent Mortgage and contain the disclosures required by
law. Nothing in this notice limits any additional notification rights you may hold under other federal or MD consumer-protection statutes applicable to the credit
transaction described above.
4. Right to a Statement of Reasons
When Fairway Independent Mortgage takes adverse action on an application for credit, or on an existing account, you have the right under the Equal Credit
Opportunity Act to a statement of the specific reasons for that action. Adverse action includes a denial or revocation of credit, a change in the terms of an
Retain this document with your loan file records. Page 1 of 3

existing account that is not favorable to Catherine Nelson and that is not applied to all or substantially all of a class of the creditor's accounts, and a refusal to
grant credit in substantially the amount or on substantially the terms requested in an application.
The statement of specific reasons will either be provided automatically with the notice of adverse action or be made available upon your timely request. If the
reasons are not disclosed in the initial notice, Fairway Independent Mortgage will include a statement of your right to request the reasons within sixty (60) days
of the notification, together with the name, address, and telephone number of the person or office from which the reasons may be obtained. Upon a timely oral
or written request, Fairway Independent Mortgage will furnish the specific reasons in writing within a reasonable period of time, generally not exceeding thirty
(30) days.
The reasons disclosed will be specific and will indicate the principal factors that influenced the action taken; statements that the action was based on internal
standards or policies, or that the applicant failed to achieve a qualifying score, are not by themselves sufficient. Any statement of reasons furnished in
connection with loan number 6061178222 will accurately reflect the actual basis upon which the credit decision was made.
5. Adverse Action and Credit Scoring
If Fairway Independent Mortgage bases its credit decision in whole or in part on information obtained from a consumer reporting agency, the notice of adverse
action provided to Catherine Nelson will identify the agency that furnished the report, including its name, address, and telephone number, and will state that the
agency did not make the credit decision and is unable to provide the specific reasons for it. The notice will further advise you of your right to obtain a free copy
of your consumer report from the reporting agency within the period prescribed by the Fair Credit Reporting Act and of your right to dispute the accuracy or
completeness of information contained in that report.
Where the credit decision is based in whole or in part on a credit score obtained from a consumer reporting agency, Fairway Independent Mortgage will
disclose the score used, the range of possible scores, the key factors that adversely affected the score, the date the score was created, and the name of the
entity that provided the score, to the extent required by applicable law. These disclosures are intended to enable Catherine Nelson to understand the role that
scored information played in the action taken on the application bearing loan number 6061178222.
If Fairway Independent Mortgage obtains information from an outside source other than a consumer reporting agency, you have the right to make a written
request, within sixty (60) days of receiving the adverse-action notice, for disclosure of the nature of that information. Fairway Independent Mortgage will
respond to any such request in accordance with the procedures and timing established under the Fair Credit Reporting Act and Regulation B.
6. Government Monitoring Information
For applications to purchase or refinance a dwelling occupied or to be occupied as a principal residence, federal law requires Fairway Independent Mortgage to
request certain information regarding the applicant's ethnicity, race, and sex for the purpose of monitoring compliance with the Equal Credit Opportunity Act, the
Home Mortgage Disclosure Act, and other fair-lending statutes. The furnishing of this information by Catherine Nelson is voluntary, and Fairway Independent
Mortgage may not discriminate on the basis of this information, or on the basis of whether you choose to provide it, in any aspect of the credit transaction.
If you choose not to furnish the requested monitoring information, federal regulations may require Fairway Independent Mortgage to note the information on the
basis of visual observation, surname, or both, for an application taken in person. This collection is undertaken solely for governmental monitoring and statistical
purposes and plays no part in the evaluation of the creditworthiness of Catherine Nelson or in the decision to extend, deny, or condition the credit associated
with loan number 6061178222.
Monitoring information collected by Fairway Independent Mortgage is maintained, reported, and safeguarded in accordance with applicable federal and MD law
and is segregated from the factors used in underwriting to the extent required. Fairway Independent Mortgage will not use this information for any purpose
inconsistent with the regulatory objectives for which it is gathered.
7. Appraisals and Valuations
In connection with an application for credit to be secured by a first lien on a dwelling, Fairway Independent Mortgage will provide Catherine Nelson with copies
of all appraisals and other written valuations developed in connection with the application, whether or not the credit is ultimately extended. Such copies will be
furnished promptly upon completion, or within the period prescribed by Regulation B before consummation or account opening, whichever is earlier, regardless
of whether the application results in a closed transaction.
You may waive the timing requirement for receiving these copies in advance, provided that Fairway Independent Mortgage delivers the copies at or before

In evaluating your application, Fairway Independent Mortgage may request only the information that is permissible under Regulation B and that is reasonably

race, color, religion, national origin, or sex except for the limited monitoring purposes described above or as otherwise permitted by law, and it will not inquire

The federal agency that administers compliance with the Equal Credit Opportunity Act with respect to Fairway Independent Mortgage is identified in the notice

name and mailing address will be disclosed in the relevant notice furnished to Catherine Nelson.

provided by statute, subject to the limitation periods established by law.

Fairway Independent Mortgage retains applications, notices, and supporting documentation relating to the credit transaction identified above for the periods

5. Obtaining Paper Copies

Even after you consent to electronic delivery, you retain the right to request and receive a paper copy of any Communication that we have provided or made available to you electronically. To request a paper copy, you may contact us by telephone at 923-674-2387x4208 or by writing to us at 124 Miranda Lights Suite 507, West Crystalhaven, ID 39766, in each case identifying yourself, referencing loan number 6061178222, and specifying the Communication or Communications you wish to receive in paper form. We will provide the requested paper copy within a reasonable time after receiving your request.

We may, to the extent permitted by applicable law, charge a reasonable fee for providing paper copies of Communications that were delivered electronically; any such fee, if applicable, will be disclosed to you in advance. Requesting a paper copy of a particular Communication will not, by itself, be treated as a withdrawal of your consent to receive Communications electronically, and your consent will remain in effect for all other Communications unless you withdraw it in accordance with the procedures described in this Consent.

6. Keeping Your Contact Information Current

It is your responsibility to provide us with a true, accurate, and complete email address and other contact information, and to keep that information current at all times so that we are able to deliver electronic Communications to you. You agree to promptly notify us of any change to your email address, mailing address, telephone number, or other contact information. Should you notify us of a change and we have had a reasonable opportunity to act upon it, we are entitled to rely on the most recent contact information in our records as the proper address for delivery of Communications.

You may update your contact information by telephone at 923-674-2387x4208, by writing to us at 124 Miranda Lights Suite 507, West Crystalhaven, ID 39766, or, if available, through the secure online portal or mobile application associated with your loan. If an electronic Communication is returned to us as undeliverable, or if we otherwise become aware that you are not receiving electronic Communications at the address you provided, we may, in our discretion, suspend electronic delivery and resume delivering Communications on paper to your address of record until valid electronic contact information is reestablished.

You agree that an electronic Communication is deemed received by you when it is sent to the email address in our records or when it is posted and made available to you through the applicable website, portal, or application, regardless of whether you have actually accessed or opened the Communication. Failure to maintain accurate contact information does not relieve you of responsibility for any Communication properly directed to you.

7. Withdrawing Your Consent

You have the right to withdraw your consent to receive Communications electronically at any time. To withdraw your consent, you must notify us by telephone at 923-674-2387x4208 or in writing at 124 Miranda Lights Suite 507, West Crystalhaven, ID 39766, identifying yourself, referencing loan number 6061178222 and clearly stating that you wish to withdraw your consent to electronic Communications. Your withdrawal will become effective only after we have had a reasonable period of time to process your request, and any Communication delivered electronically before your withdrawal takes effect will be deemed validly delivered.

Withdrawing your consent will not invalidate or affect the legal effectiveness, validity, or enforceability of any Communication or electronically signed document that was provided or executed before the withdrawal became effective. After your withdrawal takes effect, we will provide Communications to you in paper form at your address of record. We will not impose any fee or penalty solely as a consequence of your decision to withdraw consent, although other charges that otherwise apply to your loan or account, independent of your communication preference, may continue to apply.

8. Federal and State Law Governing This Consent

This Consent and the electronic delivery of Communications and the use of electronic signatures contemplated by it are made pursuant to, and are intended to comply with, the Uniform Electronic Signatures Act and, where applicable, the version of the Uniform Electronic Transactions Act or comparable electronic records statute in effect in IN. You acknowledge that, where the law requires that certain information be provided in writing, your consent permits that information to be provided electronically in a form that you can retain.

Nothing in this Consent is intended to require electronic delivery or electronic signing of any record where applicable law expressly prohibits delivery or execution in electronic form, and no such record will be delivered or executed by a method permitted by law. The provisions of this Consent are in addition to, and do not replace or limit, any other consent, authorization, or agreement you have provided or may provide to us in connection with your loan or your relationship with us.

9. Records Retention and Reliability

We will retain electronic records of Communications and of documents you sign electronically in a form that accurately reflects the information contained in each record and that remains accessible to those entitled to access it for the period required by applicable law. Such electronic records, including any associated metadata, transaction logs, and authentication data, will constitute the official records of the underlying Communication or agreement and may be relied upon and enforced by us, our affiliates, and our successors and assigns to the same extent as an original paper record.

You are encouraged to download, print, or otherwise save your own copies of Communications and signed documents at the time they are presented to you, both because electronic access through a website, portal, or application may not continue indefinitely and because retaining your own copies provides an independent record for your files. You agree that the absence of a separately retained copy on your part will not affect the validity or enforceability of any Communication or document that we have properly delivered or that you have properly executed.

10. Acknowledgment and Agreement

By providing your consent electronically in connection with loan number 6061178222, you acknowledge and agree that you have received and read this Consent; that you are able to access, view, retain, and print or save Communications in the electronic forms described above; that you consent to receive Communications electronically and to use electronic signatures on the terms set forth in this Consent; and that you understand your rights to obtain paper copies.

SELLER shall deliver possession of the Property to BUYER at closing, free of all occupants, with and place as the parties may mutually agree, SELLER shall deliver possession of the Property to BUYER at closing, free of all occupants, with all systems and fixtures in working order. 6. CONTINGENCIES (a) Financing Contingency. This Agreement is contingent upon BUYER obtaining a firm written mortgage loan commitment in the approximate amount of $300,739.00 at prevailing market rates and terms. If BUYER, after a good-faith effort, is unable to obtain such commitment, BUYER may terminate this Agreement and the earnest money shall be refunded. (b) Inspection Contingency. This Agreement is contingent upon BUYER's right, at BUYER's expense, to have the Property inspected by licensed inspectors of BUYER's choosing. Should inspections reveal material defects, BUYER may request repairs, renegotiate the price, or terminate this Agreement within the inspection period. (c) Title Contingency. Page 1 of 2

SELLER shall convey good and marketable title by general warranty deed, free and clear of all liens and encumbrances except those of record acceptable to BUYER. Title shall be insured through Nova Title Company. BUYER may terminate this Agreement if title defects are not timely cured.

DEFAULT

If BUYER defaults, SELLER may retain the earnest money as liquidated damages. If SELLER defaults, BUYER may pursue specific earnest money together with any actual damages permitted by law.

8. ACCEPTANCE AND SIGNATURES
